title: 3 Bedroom Houses for Rent in West Park, Cleveland
metaTitle: 3 Bedroom Houses for Rent in West Park, Cleveland OH
metaDescription: Searching for 3 bedroom houses for rent in West Park, Cleveland? See the neighborhood's rent ranges, RTA West Park station, and how to tell us what you need.
primaryKeyword: 3 bedroom houses for rent West Park cleveland
answerBox: West Park, on Cleveland's far west side, includes the Kamm's Corners and Jefferson areas and is served by its own RTA Red Line station. Citywide, three-bedroom rentals average around $1,350 a month per Zumper's July 2026 report; contact Rent Finder Cleveland to tell us what you're looking for in this part of the city.

sections.h2: 3-bedroom houses for rent in West Park
body: West Park covers Cleveland's far west side, made up of the **Kamm's Corners** and **Jefferson** planning areas near the city's border with Fairview Park and North Olmsted. Housing here includes early-to-mid-1900s single-family houses, many with three bedrooms, on side streets branching off Lorain Road and Rocky River Drive, along with pockets of mid-century construction closer to the city line.

Rent Finder Cleveland's rental selection is concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast neighborhoods, with a smaller number of homes on the west side. We don't currently have a standing inventory to publish specifically for West Park, but if you're set on this part of the city, [contact us](/p/book-showing) and tell us what you need — we'll let you know if anything in our current selection is a match.

sections.h2: How much does a 3-bedroom house cost in West Park?
body: West Park doesn't have its own published rent figures in our data, so the most reliable benchmark is Cleveland's citywide market. Three-bedroom rentals citywide averaged around $1,350 a month as of Zumper's July 2026 report, while RentCafe's July 2026 figures for larger, professionally managed buildings show three-bedrooms averaging closer to $2,716 — a different, newer segment of the market than most West Park houses.

West Park has its own **West Park** RTA Red Line rapid station, one of the few Cleveland neighborhoods outside downtown and University Circle with a dedicated rail stop, giving direct access to downtown's Tower City and to Cleveland Hopkins International Airport in the other direction. Kamm's Corners, the commercial node at Lorain Road and Rocky River Drive, anchors the neighborhood's shopping and dining, and Jefferson's residential streets sit just south along the same Lorain Road corridor.

items: **Transit:** West Park RTA Red Line station; local bus routes along Lorain Rd
**Highways:** Near I-480 and close to Cleveland Hopkins International Airport, useful for a car commute across the west side
**Parks:** Cleveland Metroparks' Rocky River Reservation and West Creek Reservation, both within a short drive
**Commercial hub:** Kamm's Corners at Lorain Rd and Rocky River Dr

sections.h2: Section 8 and vouchers on Cleveland's west side
body: Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with — wherever it's located — accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready, though our current west-side inventory is limited compared to the east and southeast side of the city. Voucher holders looking specifically in West Park should know that CMHA, the county's public housing authority, accepts preliminary applications year-round and selects applicants through periodic mini-lotteries rather than a strict waitlist order. Ohio also has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept vouchers, and Cleveland itself has no local source-of-income protection ordinance. Read more in our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/).

sections.h2: Older housing stock and lead-safe certification
body: Much of West Park's housing, including the Kamm's Corners and Jefferson areas, dates to the early-to-mid 1900s, before lead-based paint was banned nationwide in 1978. Cleveland Codified Ordinances §365.04 requires any residential rental unit in the city built before January 1, 1978 to hold a current Lead-Safe Certification, renewed every two years after an independent evaluation. Whichever west-side landlord you rent from, it's worth confirming this certification is current before signing a lease.

sections.h2: Looking for a 3-bedroom house near West Park?
body: If a West Park address specifically isn't essential, our broader [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) listings cover the neighborhoods where we work with the most Section 8-friendly homes, primarily on the city's east and southeast side. Otherwise, [reach out and describe what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) — bedroom count, budget, and whether you have a voucher — and we'll follow up as matching inventory becomes available across our Cleveland-area selection.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland have 3-bedroom houses for rent in West Park?
faq.a: Our current selection is concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast neighborhoods, with fewer homes on the west side, so we don't have standing West Park inventory to list. Contact us with what you're looking for and we'll let you know about any matching availability.

faq.q: What neighborhoods make up West Park?
faq.a: West Park is made up of the Kamm's Corners and Jefferson planning areas on Cleveland's far west side, near the city's border with Fairview Park and North Olmsted. Kamm's Corners, at Lorain Road and Rocky River Drive, is the neighborhood's main commercial hub, with Jefferson's residential streets extending south along the same corridor.

faq.q: How much does a 3-bedroom house rent for in West Park?
faq.a: West Park doesn't have separately published rent data, but citywide, Cleveland's three-bedroom rentals averaged around $1,350 a month as of Zumper's July 2026 report. Larger, professionally managed buildings run higher, closer to $2,716 a month per RentCafe's July 2026 data.

faq.q: Is there RTA rail access in West Park?
faq.a: Yes. West Park has its own RTA Red Line rapid station, giving direct access to downtown Cleveland's Tower City and to Cleveland Hopkins International Airport. It's one of the few Cleveland neighborhoods outside downtown and University Circle with a dedicated rail stop.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland accept Section 8 in West Park?
faq.a: Every home we work with accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, wherever it's located, though our current west-side inventory is smaller than our east-side selection. Ohio law doesn't require all landlords to accept vouchers, and Cleveland has no local source-of-income protection ordinance.
